regex.h(3) Library Functions Manual regex.h(3)

NAME

regex.h - PCRE2-based regex patterns for cryptographic parsing.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


bool crypto_regex_match_known_hosts (const char *line, char **ip_port_out, char **key_type_out, char **hex_key_out, char **comment_out)
bool crypto_regex_match_public_key (const char *line, char **base64_key_out, char **comment_out)
bool crypto_regex_extract_pem_base64 (const char *file_content, char **base64_data_out)
bool crypto_regex_extract_gpg_keygrip (const char *line, char **keygrip_out)

Detailed Description

PCRE2-based regex patterns for cryptographic parsing.

Unified module for regex-based parsing of SSH-related formats:

  • SSH known_hosts file entries
  • SSH Ed25519 public key lines
  • OpenSSH private key PEM format

Uses PCRE2

Definition in file regex.h.

Function Documentation

bool crypto_regex_extract_gpg_keygrip (const char * line, char ** keygrip_out)

Extract GPG keygrip from colon-delimited GPG output

Format: grp:::::::::D52FF935FBA59609EE65E1685287828242A1EA1A: Where the keygrip is a 40-character hexadecimal string after 8 colon-delimited empty fields

Parameters

line Input line from GPG output (must start with "grp:")
keygrip_out Output: allocated string with 40-char hex keygrip

Returns

true if line matches GPG keygrip format, false otherwise

Note

Caller must free output string with SAFE_FREE()

Returns false if regex is not available (caller should use fallback manual parsing)

Definition at line 262 of file regex.c.

262                                                                             {
263   if (!line || !keygrip_out) {
264     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
265     return false;
266   }
267 
268   pcre2_code *regex = crypto_regex_get_gpg_keygrip();
269   if (!regex) {
270     /* Regex not available - caller should use fallback manual parsing */
271     return false;
272   }
273 
274   pcre2_match_data *match_data = pcre2_match_data_create_from_pattern(regex, NULL);
275   if (!match_data) {
276     return false;
277   }
278 
279   /* Perform JIT match (falls back to interpreted if JIT unavailable) */
280   int rc = pcre2_jit_match(regex, (PCRE2_SPTR)line, strlen(line), 0, /* startoffset */
281                            0,                                        /* options */
282                            match_data, NULL);                        /* mcontext */
283 
284   if (rc < 0) {
285     pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
286     return false;
287   }
288 
289   /* Extract keygrip from named group */
290   *keygrip_out = asciichat_pcre2_extract_named_group(regex, match_data, "keygrip", line);
291 
292   pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
293 
294   return *keygrip_out != NULL;
295 }




References asciichat_pcre2_extract_named_group(),
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by gpg_get_public_key().






bool crypto_regex_extract_pem_base64 (const char *
  file_content, char ** base64_data_out)


Extract base64 data from OpenSSH private key PEM format




Format: -----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY----- <multiline base64
    data> -----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----




Parameters


file_content Full contents of PEM key file


base64_data_out Output: allocated string with base64 data (includes
  whitespace)


Returns


true if PEM structure found and extracted, false
  otherwise


Note


Caller must free output string with SAFE_FREE()



Output includes newlines and whitespace (caller should remove as
    needed)




Definition at line 227 of file regex.c.


227                                                                                        {
228   if (!file_content || !base64_data_out) {
229     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
230     return false;
231   }
232 
233   pcre2_code *regex = crypto_regex_get_openssh_pem();
234   if (!regex) {
235     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid validator state");
236     return false;
237   }
238 
239   pcre2_match_data *match_data = pcre2_match_data_create_from_pattern(regex, NULL);
240   if (!match_data) {
241     return false;
242   }
243 
244   /* Perform JIT match (falls back to interpreted if JIT unavailable) */
245   int rc = pcre2_jit_match(regex, (PCRE2_SPTR)file_content, strlen(file_content), 0, /* startoffset */
246                            0,                                                        /* options */
247                            match_data, NULL);                                        /* mcontext */
248 
249   if (rc < 0) {
250     pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
251     return false;
252   }
253 
254   /* Extract base64 data */
255   *base64_data_out = asciichat_pcre2_extract_named_group(regex, match_data, "base64_data", file_content);
256 
257   pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
258 
259   return *base64_data_out != NULL;
260 }






References asciichat_pcre2_extract_named_group(),
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, and
    SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by parse_ssh_private_key().






bool crypto_regex_match_known_hosts (const char * line,
  char ** ip_port_out, char ** key_type_out, char ** hex_key_out, char **
  comment_out)


Match SSH known_hosts line and extract components




Format: <IP:port> <keytype> <hex_key> [comment]
    Example: 192.0.2.1:8080 x25519 0123456789abcdef... ascii-chat




Parameters


line Input line from known_hosts file


ip_port_out Output: allocated string with IP:port (e.g.,
  "192.0.2.1:8080")


key_type_out Output: allocated string with key type (e.g.,
  "x25519", "no-identity")


hex_key_out Output: allocated string with 64-char hex key (may be NULL
  for no-identity)


comment_out Output: allocated string with comment (may be NULL)


Returns


true if line matches known_hosts format, false
  otherwise


Note


Caller must free all non-NULL output strings with
  SAFE_FREE()



Returns false if any output pointer is NULL




Definition at line 136 of file regex.c.


137                                                         {
138   if (!line || !ip_port_out || !key_type_out || !hex_key_out || !comment_out) {
139     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
140     return false;
141   }
142 
143   pcre2_code *regex = crypto_regex_get_known_hosts();
144   if (!regex) {
145     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid validator state");
146     return false;
147   }
148 
149   pcre2_match_data *match_data = pcre2_match_data_create_from_pattern(regex, NULL);
150   if (!match_data) {
151     return false;
152   }
153 
154   /* Perform JIT match (falls back to interpreted if JIT unavailable) */
155   int rc = pcre2_jit_match(regex, (PCRE2_SPTR)line, strlen(line), 0, /* startoffset */
156                            0,                                        /* options */
157                            match_data, NULL);                        /* mcontext */
158 
159   if (rc < 0) {
160     pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
161     return false;
162   }
163 
164   /* Extract named groups */
165   *ip_port_out = asciichat_pcre2_extract_named_group(regex, match_data, "ip_port", line);
166   *key_type_out = asciichat_pcre2_extract_named_group(regex, match_data, "key_type", line);
167   *hex_key_out = asciichat_pcre2_extract_named_group(regex, match_data, "hex_key", line);
168   *comment_out = asciichat_pcre2_extract_named_group(regex, match_data, "comment", line);
169 
170   pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
171 
172   /* Verify we got required fields */
173   if (!*ip_port_out || !*key_type_out) {
174     SAFE_FREE(*ip_port_out);
175     SAFE_FREE(*key_type_out);
176     SAFE_FREE(*hex_key_out);
177     SAFE_FREE(*comment_out);
178     return false;
179   }
180 
181   return true;
182 }






References asciichat_pcre2_extract_named_group(),
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, SAFE_FREE,
    and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by check_known_host(), and
    check_known_host_no_identity().






bool crypto_regex_match_public_key (const char * line,
  char ** base64_key_out, char ** comment_out)


Match SSH Ed25519 public key line and extract base64




Format: ssh-ed25519 <base64_key> [comment] Example:
    ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAI... test-key




Parameters


line Input line from SSH public key file


base64_key_out Output: allocated string with base64-encoded key


comment_out Output: allocated string with comment (may be NULL)


Returns


true if line matches SSH Ed25519 format, false
  otherwise


Note


Caller must free output strings with SAFE_FREE()



Case-insensitive: matches "SSH-ED25519" or
    "ssh-ed25519"




Definition at line 184 of file regex.c.


184                                                                                                 {
185   if (!line || !base64_key_out || !comment_out) {
186     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
187     return false;
188   }
189 
190   pcre2_code *regex = crypto_regex_get_ssh_public_key();
191   if (!regex) {
192     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid validator state");
193     return false;
194   }
195 
196   pcre2_match_data *match_data = pcre2_match_data_create_from_pattern(regex, NULL);
197   if (!match_data) {
198     return false;
199   }
200 
201   /* Perform JIT match (falls back to interpreted if JIT unavailable) */
202   int rc = pcre2_jit_match(regex, (PCRE2_SPTR)line, strlen(line), 0, /* startoffset */
203                            0,                                        /* options */
204                            match_data, NULL);                        /* mcontext */
205 
206   if (rc < 0) {
207     pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
208     return false;
209   }
210 
211   /* Extract named groups */
212   *base64_key_out = asciichat_pcre2_extract_named_group(regex, match_data, "base64_key", line);
213   *comment_out = asciichat_pcre2_extract_named_group(regex, match_data, "comment", line);
214 
215   pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
216 
217   /* Verify we got required field */
218   if (!*base64_key_out) {
219     SAFE_FREE(*base64_key_out);
220     SAFE_FREE(*comment_out);
221     return false;
222   }
223 
224   return true;
225 }






References asciichat_pcre2_extract_named_group(),
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, SAFE_FREE,
    and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by parse_ssh_ed25519_line().
